reasoning: The 30-year fixed rate jumbo mortgage index (OBMMIJUMBO30YF) stood at 6.451% on November 21, 2025, down from a recent high of 6.671% earlier in the month. The FRED statistical tool projects a short-term upward trend, calculating a 0.67 probability of the index being higher than 6.451% by November 30. Over the short horizon, volatility and the recent local low provide room for a technical rebound, supporting a ~0.64 probability. As the horizon extends to December and February, industry forecasts (MBA, Fannie Mae) predicting a gradual decline in mortgage rates through 2025 and into 2026 exert downward pressure on the base expectation. Furthermore, according to constraints, uncertainty must increase over time—hence the probabilities regress toward 0.5 for the increasingly distant dates (0.56 for Dec 23, and 0.52 for Feb 21).
